Interface OutboxInsert

All Superinterfaces:
AutoCloseable
public interface OutboxInsert extends AutoCloseable
Per-batch insert handle returned by OutboxDialect.prepareInsert(Connection, TableRef). The dialect owns the underlying PreparedStatement and the parameter order; the publisher only ever sees the OutboxEvent / resolved id surface defined here.

Lifecycle:

  1. OutboxDialect.prepareInsert(Connection, TableRef) returns a handle bound to an open connection.
  2. The publisher calls bind(OutboxEvent, UUID) followed by addBatch() once per event.
  3. The publisher calls executeBatch() once when the batch is complete.
  4. The publisher always calls close() via try-with-resources, even on failure.

Implementations are not required to be thread-safe; a fresh handle is acquired for every publish / publishAll call.

Since:
0.2.0

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    addBatch()
    Adds the currently-bound row to the batch.
    void
    bind(OutboxEvent event, UUID resolvedId)
    Binds the event to the statement using the dialect's chosen parameter order.
    void
    close()
    Releases the underlying PreparedStatement.
    int[]
    executeBatch()
    Executes the accumulated batch and returns the per-row update counts.

  • Method Details

    • bind

      void bind(OutboxEvent event, UUID resolvedId) throws SQLException
      Binds the event to the statement using the dialect's chosen parameter order. The resolvedId is the id the publisher elected to persist — either the one carried by the event or a freshly generated UUID.
      Throws:
      SQLException

    • addBatch

      void addBatch() throws SQLException
      Adds the currently-bound row to the batch.
      Throws:
      SQLException

    • executeBatch

      int[] executeBatch() throws SQLException
      Executes the accumulated batch and returns the per-row update counts.
      Throws:
      SQLException

    • close

      void close() throws SQLException
      Releases the underlying PreparedStatement.
      Specified by:
      close in interface AutoCloseable
      Throws:
      SQLException
